name: Pickled Hot Peppers
recipeIngredient: 4 pounds Hot long peppers (red, green, or yellow)
3 pounds Sweet red and green peppers (mixed)
recipeInstructions:
(Hungarian, Banana, Chile, Jalapeno)
Yield: About 9 pints
Caution: Wear rubber gloves when handling hot peppers or wash hands thoroughly with soap and water before touching your face.
Procedure:
1. Wash the peppers.
2. If small peppers are left whole, slash 2 to 4 slits in each.
3. Quarter large peppers.
4. Blanch the peppers in boiling water or blister them until the skins blister.
- Oven or broiler method: Place peppers in a hot oven (400°F) or broiler for 6-8 minutes or until skins blister.
- Range-top method: Cover a hot burner (gas or electric) with heavy wire mesh, place peppers on the burner for several minutes until skins blister.
5. Cool and peel off the skin. Flatten small peppers.
6. Fill jars, leaving 1/2-inch headspace.
7. Combine the remaining ingredients in a pot, bring to a boil, and simmer for 10 minutes.
8. Remove garlic.
9. Add the hot pickling solution over the peppers, leaving 1/2-inch headspace.
10. Adjust lids and process according to the recommendations in Table 1.
Table 1. Recommended processing time for Pickled Hot Peppers in a boiling-water canner.
Style of Pack: Raw
Jar Size: Half-pints or Pints
Process Time at Altitudes of:
0 - 1,000 ft: 10 minutes
1,001 - 6,000 ft: 15 minutes
Above 6,000 ft: 20 minutes
